title: Ophrys litigiosa
taxonomy: Ophrys litigiosa belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification is due to its orchid-like appearance and growth habits. The genus Ophrys is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as bee orchids, which are known for their unique flowers that mimic the appearance of female bees to attract male bees for pollination. Ophrys litigiosa is a species within this genus, characterized by its distinctive flowers and growth patterns. The species is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas.

This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues.
